sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id UInt32, date_column String, value Int32);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, date_column, value) VALUES (1, '2023-10-01T12:00:00.000Z', 100), (2, 'invalid-date', 200), (3, '', 300);

SELECT id, parseDateTime64InJodaSyntax(date_column, 3) AS parsed_date FROM test_dates WHERE parsed_date GLOBAL NOT IN (SELECT parseDateTime64InJodaSyntax(date_column, 3) FROM test_dates WHERE value > 150);
CREATE TABLE test_dates_extended (id UInt32, timestamp_column DateTime64(3), some_value Int32, FOREIGN KEY (id) REFERENCES test_dates(id));
SELECT a.id, a.value, a.date_column, b.timestamp_column FROM test_dates a JOIN test_dates_extended b ON a.id = b.id WHERE b.some_value GLOBAL NOT IN (SELECT value FROM test_dates WHERE id = 2);
SELECT id, parseDateTime64InJodaSyntax(date_column, 3) AS date_converted FROM test_dates GROUP BY date_converted HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE test_fuzzing (id UInt32, mix_column String);
SELECT id, parseDateTime64InJodaSyntax(mix_column, 3) AS mix_parsed FROM test_fuzzing WHERE mix_parsed IS NOT NULL GLOBAL NOT IN (SELECT parseDateTime64InJodaSyntax('2000-01-01T00:00:00.000Z', 3));
SELECT parseDateTime64InJodaSyntax(date_column, 3) AS parsed_date FROM test_dates WHERE parsed_date IS NULL OR parsed_date > parseDateTime64InJodaSyntax('2023-01-01T00:00:00.000Z', 3);
ALTER TABLE test_dates_extended ADD COLUMN new_column String DEFAULT 'default_value';
